# Skill: test-driven-development

# Test-Driven Development (TDD)

## Overview

Write the test first. Watch it fail. Write minimal code to pass.

**Core principle:** If you didn't watch the test fail, you don't know if it tests the right thing.

**Violating the letter of the rules is violating the spirit of the rules.**

## When to Use

**Always:**
- New features
- Bug fixes
- Refactoring
- Behavior changes

**Exceptions (ask your human partner):**
- Throwaway prototypes
- Generated code
- Configuration files

Thinking "skip TDD just this once"? Stop. That's rationalization.

## The Iron Law

```
NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST
```

Write code before the test? Delete it. Start over.

**No exceptions:**
- Don't keep it as "reference"
- Don't "adapt" it while writing tests
- Don't look at it
- Delete means delete

Implement fresh from tests. Period.

## Red-Green-Refactor

```dot
digraph tdd_cycle {
    rankdir=LR;
    red [label="RED\nWrite failing test", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ffcccc"];
    verify_red [label="Verify fails\ncorrectly", shape=diamond];
    green [label="GREEN\nMinimal code", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ccffcc"];
    verify_green [label="Verify passes\nAll green", shape=diamond];
    refactor [label="REFACTOR\nClean up", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ccccff"];
    next [label="Next", shape=ellipse];

    red -> verify_red;
    verify_red -> green [label="yes"];
    verify_red -> red [label="wrong\nfailure"];
    green -> verify_green;
    verify_green -> refactor [label="yes"];
    verify_green -> green [label="no"];
    refactor -> verify_green [label="stay\ngreen"];
    verify_green -> next;
    next -> red;
}
```

### RED - Write Failing Test

Write one minimal test showing what should happen.

<Good>
```typescript
test('retries failed operations 3 times', async () => {
  let attempts = 0;
  const operation = () => {
    attempts++;
    if (attempts < 3) throw new Error('fail');
    return 'success';
  };

  const result = await retryOperation(operation);

  expect(result).toBe('success');
  expect(attempts).toBe(3);
});
```
Clear name, tests real behavior, one thing
</Good>

<Bad>
```typescript
test('retry works', async () => {
  const mock = jest.fn()
    .mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error())
    .mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error())
    .mockResolvedValueOnce('success');
  await retryOperation(mock);
  expect(mock).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(3);
});
```
Vague name, tests mock not code
</Bad>

**Requirements:**
- One behavior
- Clear name
- Real code (no mocks unless unavoidable)

### Verify RED - Watch It Fail

**MANDATORY. Never skip.**

```bash
npm test path/to/test.test.ts
```

Confirm:
- Test fails (not errors)
- Failure message is expected
- Fails because feature missing (not typos)

**Test passes?** You're testing existing behavior. Fix test.

**Test errors?** Fix error, re-run until it fails correctly.

### GREEN - Minimal Code

Write simplest code to pass the test.

<Good>
```typescript
async function retryOperation<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
  for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
    try {
      return await fn();
    } catch (e) {
      if (i === 2) throw e;
    }
  }
  throw new Error('unreachable');
}
```
Just enough to pass
</Good>

<Bad>
```typescript
async function retryOperation<T>(
  fn: () => Promise<T>,
  options?: {
    maxRetries?: number;
    backoff?: 'linear' | 'exponential';
    onRetry?: (attempt: number) => void;
  }
): Promise<T> {
  // YAGNI
}
```
Over-engineered
</Bad>

Don't add features, refactor other code, or "improve" beyond the test.

### Verify GREEN - Watch It Pass

**MANDATORY.**

```bash
npm test path/to/test.test.ts
```

Confirm:
- Test passes
- Other tests still pass
- Output pristine (no errors, warnings)

**Test fails?** Fix code, not test.

**Other tests fail?** Fix now.

### REFACTOR - Clean Up

After green only:
- Remove duplication
- Improve names
- Extract helpers

Keep tests green. Don't add behavior.

### Repeat

Next failing test for next feature.

## Good Tests

| Quality | Good | Bad |
|---------|------|-----|
| **Minimal** | One thing. "and" in name? Split it. | `test('validates email and domain and whitespace')` |
| **Clear** | Name describes behavior | `test('test1')` |
| **Shows intent** | Demonstrates desired API | Obscures what code should do |

## Why Order Matters

**"I'll write tests after to verify it works"**

Tests written after code pass immediately. Passing immediately proves nothing:
- Might test wrong thing
- Might test implementation, not behavior
- Might miss edge cases you forgot
- You never saw it catch the bug

Test-first forces you to see the test fail, proving it actually tests something.

**"I already manually tested all the edge cases"**

Manual testing is ad-hoc. You think you tested everything but:
- No record of what you tested
- Can't re-run when code changes
- Easy to forget cases under pressure
- "It worked when I tried it" ≠ comprehensive

Automated tests are systematic. They run the same way every time.

**"Deleting X hours of work is wasteful"**

Sunk cost fallacy. The time is already gone. Your choice now:
- Delete and rewrite with TDD (X more hours, high confidence)
- Keep it and add tests after (30 min, low confidence, likely bugs)

The "waste" is keeping code you can't trust. Working code without real tests is technical debt.

**"TDD is dogmatic, being pragmatic means adapting"**

TDD IS pragmatic:
- Finds bugs before commit (faster than debugging after)
- Prevents regressions (tests catch breaks immediately)
- Documents behavior (tests show how to use code)
- Enables refactoring (change freely, tests catch breaks)

"Pragmatic" shortcuts = debugging in production = slower.

**"Tests after achieve the same goals - it's spirit not ritual"**

No. Tests-after answer "What does this do?" Tests-first answer "What should this do?"

Tests-after are biased by your implementation. You test what you built, not what's required. You verify remembered edge cases, not discovered ones.

Tests-first force edge case discovery before implementing. Tests-after verify you remembered everything (you didn't).

30 minutes of tests after ≠ TDD. You get coverage, lose proof tests work.

## Common Rationalizations

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Too simple to test" | Simple code breaks. Test takes 30 seconds. |
| "I'll test after" | Tests passing immediately prove nothing. |
| "Tests after achieve same goals" | Tests-after = "what does this do?" Tests-first = "what should this do?" |
| "Already manually tested" | Ad-hoc ≠ systematic. No record, can't re-run. |
| "Deleting X hours is wasteful" | Sunk cost fallacy. Keeping unverified code is technical debt. |
| "Keep as reference, write tests first" | You'll adapt it. That's testing after. Delete means delete. |
| "Need to explore first" | Fine. Throw away exploration, start with TDD. |
| "Test hard = design unclear" | Listen to test. Hard to test = hard to use. |
| "TDD will slow me down" | TDD faster than debugging. Pragmatic = test-first. |
| "Manual test faster" | Manual doesn't prove edge cases. You'll re-test every change. |
| "Existing code has no tests" | You're improving it. Add tests for existing code. |

## Red Flags - STOP and Start Over

- Code before test
- Test after implementation
- Test passes immediately
- Can't explain why test failed
- Tests added "later"
- Rationalizing "just this once"
- "I already manually tested it"
- "Tests after achieve the same purpose"
- "It's about spirit not ritual"
- "Keep as reference" or "adapt existing code"
- "Already spent X hours, deleting is wasteful"
- "TDD is dogmatic, I'm being pragmatic"
- "This is different because..."

**All of these mean: Delete code. Start over with TDD.**

## Example: Bug Fix

**Bug:** Empty email accepted

**RED**
```typescript
test('rejects empty email', async () => {
  const result = await submitForm({ email: '' });
  expect(result.error).toBe('Email required');
});
```

**Verify RED**
```bash
$ npm test
FAIL: expected 'Email required', got undefined
```

**GREEN**
```typescript
function submitForm(data: FormData) {
  if (!data.email?.trim()) {
    return { error: 'Email required' };
  }
  // ...
}
```

**Verify GREEN**
```bash
$ npm test
PASS
```

**REFACTOR**
Extract validation for multiple fields if needed.

## Verification Checklist

Before marking work complete:

- [ ] Every new function/method has a test
- [ ] Watched each test fail before implementing
- [ ] Each test failed for expected reason (feature missing, not typo)
- [ ] Wrote minimal code to pass each test
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Output pristine (no errors, warnings)
- [ ] Tests use real code (mocks only if unavoidable)
- [ ] Edge cases and errors covered

Can't check all boxes? You skipped TDD. Start over.

## When Stuck

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| Don't know how to test | Write wished-for API. Write assertion first. Ask your human partner. |
| Test too complicated | Design too complicated. Simplify interface. |
| Must mock everything | Code too coupled. Use dependency injection. |
| Test setup huge | Extract helpers. Still complex? Simplify design. |

## Debugging Integration

Bug found? Write failing test reproducing it. Follow TDD cycle. Test proves fix and prevents regression.

Never fix bugs without a test.

## Testing Anti-Patterns

When adding mocks or test utilities, read @testing-anti-patterns.md to avoid common pitfalls:
- Testing mock behavior instead of real behavior
- Adding test-only methods to production classes
- Mocking without understanding dependencies

## Final Rule

```
Production code → test exists and failed first
Otherwise → not TDD
```

No exceptions without your human partner's permission.

<content>1: // ─── Per-Session Message Cache ───────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Records every translated event per session in real-time (memory + JSONL file),
3: // replays on session switch through existing client handlers.
4: //
5: // Modeled after claude-relay's doSendAndRecord + appendToSessionFile pattern:
6: //   - Record synchronously before broadcast (identical to claude-relay)
7: //   - Append-only JSONL files (crash-safe, O(1) per event)
8: //   - Fallback chain: memory → file → null (caller uses REST API)
9: 
10: import {
11: 	appendFileSync,
12: 	mkdirSync,
13: 	readdirSync,
14: 	readFileSync,
15: 	unlinkSync,
16: 	writeFileSync,
17: } from "node:fs";
18: import { join } from "node:path";
19: import type { RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
20: 
21: /** Maximum events per session before eviction. */
22: const MAX_EVENTS = 5000;
23: 
24: /** After eviction, keep this fraction of events (newest). */
25: const KEEP_RATIO = 0.8;
26: 
27: // ─── Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28: 
29: export interface FileOpResult {
30: 	ok: boolean;
31: 	error?: string;
32: }
33: 
34: interface SessionCache {
35: 	events: RelayMessage[];
36: }
37: 
38: // ─── MessageCache ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
39: 
40: export class MessageCache {
41: 	private readonly sessions = new Map<string, SessionCache>();
42: 	private readonly cacheDir: string;
43: 
44: 	constructor(cacheDir: string) {
45: 		this.cacheDir = cacheDir;
46: 		mkdirSync(cacheDir, { recursive: true });
47: 	}
48: 
49: 	// ── Recording (real-time, identical to doSendAndRecord) ────────────
50: 
51: 	/**
52: 	 * Append event to memory + file. Called synchronously before broadcast.
53: 	 * Identical to claude-relay's doSendAndRecord pattern.
54: 	 * Evicts oldest events when MAX_EVENTS is exceeded.
55: 	 */
56: 	recordEvent(sessionId: string, event: RelayMessage): void {
57: 		const session = this.ensureSession(sessionId);
58: 		session.events.push(event);
59: 		this.appendToFile(sessionId, event);
60: 
61: 		// Evict oldest events when over limit
62: 		if (session.events.length > MAX_EVENTS) {
63: 			const keepCount = Math.floor(MAX_EVENTS * KEEP_RATIO);
64: 			session.events = session.events.slice(-keepCount);
65: 			this.rewriteFile(sessionId, session.events);
66: 		}
67: 	}
68: 
69: 	// ── Serving (fallback chain) ───────────────────────────────────────
70: 
71: 	/**
72: 	 * Get raw events for a session. Fallback chain:
73: 	 *   1. In-memory events → return if found
74: 	 *   2. Load from JSONL file on disk → return if found
75: 	 *   3. Return null → caller fetches from OpenCode REST API
76: 	 */
77: 	getEvents(sessionId: string): RelayMessage[] | null {
78: 		// 1. In-memory
79: 		const session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
80: 		if (session && session.events.length > 0) return session.events;
81: 
82: 		// 2. File on disk
83: 		const fromFile = this.loadFromFile(sessionId);
84: 		if (fromFile && fromFile.length > 0) {
85: 			this.sessions.set(sessionId, { events: fromFile });
86: 			return fromFile;
87: 		}
88: 
89: 		// 3. Return null → caller fetches from REST API
90: 		return null;
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	// ── Lifecycle ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
94: 
95: 	/** Remove all data (memory + file). Called on session delete. */
96: 	remove(sessionId: string): void {
97: 		this.sessions.delete(sessionId);
98: 		try {
99: 			unlinkSync(this.filePath(sessionId));
100: 		} catch {
101: 			// File may not exist — that's fine
102: 		}
103: 	}
104: 
105: 	/** Load all .jsonl files into memory. Called once on startup. */
106: 	loadFromDisk(): void {
107: 		let files: string[];
108: 		try {
109: 			files = readdirSync(this.cacheDir);
110: 		} catch {
111: 			return;
112: 		}
113: 
114: 		for (const file of files) {
115: 			if (!file.endsWith(".jsonl")) continue;
116: 			const sessionId = file.slice(0, -6); // Remove ".jsonl"
117: 			const events = this.loadFromFile(sessionId);
118: 			if (events && events.length > 0) {
119: 				this.sessions.set(sessionId, { events });
120: 			}
121: 		}
122: 	}
123: 
124: 	/** Check if a session has cached events in memory. */
125: 	has(sessionId: string): boolean {
126: 		const session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
127: 		return session !== undefined && session.events.length > 0;
128: 	}
129: 
130: 	/** Number of sessions currently cached. */
131: 	sessionCount(): number {
132: 		return this.sessions.size;
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	// ── Internal ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
136: 
137: 	private ensureSession(sessionId: string): SessionCache {
138: 		let session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
139: 		if (!session) {
140: 			session = { events: [] };
141: 			this.sessions.set(sessionId, session);
142: 		}
143: 		return session;
144: 	}
145: 
146: 	private filePath(sessionId: string): string {
147: 		return join(this.cacheDir, `${sessionId}.jsonl`);
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	private loadFromFile(sessionId: string): RelayMessage[] | null {
151: 		let content: string;
152: 		try {
153: 			content = readFileSync(this.filePath(sessionId), "utf8");
154: 		} catch {
155: 			return null;
156: 		}
157: 
158: 		const lines = content.trim().split("\n");
159: 		if (lines.length === 0 || (lines.length === 1 && lines[0] === "")) {
160: 			return null;
161: 		}
162: 
163: 		const events: RelayMessage[] = [];
164: 		for (const line of lines) {
165: 			if (!line) continue;
166: 			try {
167: 				events.push(JSON.parse(line) as RelayMessage);
168: 			} catch {
169: 				// Skip malformed lines (crash-safe: partial last line is OK)
170: 			}
171: 		}
172: 		return events.length > 0 ? events : null;
173: 	}
174: 
175: 	private appendToFile(sessionId: string, event: RelayMessage): FileOpResult {
176: 		try {
177: 			appendFileSync(this.filePath(sessionId), `${JSON.stringify(event)}\n`);
178: 			return { ok: true };
179: 		} catch (err) {
180: 			return { ok: false, error: (err as Error).message };
181: 		}
182: 	}
183: 
184: 	/** Rewrite the JSONL file with only the given events (after eviction). */
185: 	private rewriteFile(sessionId: string, events: RelayMessage[]): FileOpResult {
186: 		try {
187: 			const content = `${events.map((e) => JSON.stringify(e)).join("\n")}\n`;
188: 			writeFileSync(this.filePath(sessionId), content);
189: 			return { ok: true };
190: 		} catch (err) {
191: 			return { ok: false, error: (err as Error).message };
192: 		}
193: 	}
194: }

(End of file - total 194 lines)

<content>1: // ─── MessageCache Unit Tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Tests for the per-session file-backed event cache.
3: // Verifies: record/serve roundtrip, JSONL persistence, fallback chain,
4: // loadFromDisk recovery, session isolation, and deletion.
5: 
6: import {
7: 	chmodSync,
8: 	existsSync,
9: 	mkdirSync,
10: 	readFileSync,
11: 	rmSync,
12: 	writeFileSync,
13: } from "node:fs";
14: import { tmpdir } from "node:os";
15: import { join } from "node:path";
16: import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
17: import { MessageCache } from "../../src/lib/message-cache.js";
18: import type { RelayMessage } from "../../src/lib/types.js";
19: 
20: // ─── Test Helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
21: 
22: let testDir: string;
23: 
24: function createTestDir(): string {
25: 	const dir = join(
26: 		tmpdir(),
27: 		`message-cache-test-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`,
28: 	);
29: 	mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });
30: 	return dir;
31: }
32: 
33: beforeEach(() => {
34: 	testDir = createTestDir();
35: });
36: 
37: afterEach(() => {
38: 	try {
39: 		rmSync(testDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
40: 	} catch {
41: 		// Best effort cleanup
42: 	}
43: });
44: 
45: // ─── recordEvent + getEvents roundtrip ──────────────────────────────────────
46: 
47: describe("recordEvent + getEvents", () => {
48: 	it("returns events after recording them", () => {
49: 		const cache = new MessageCache(testDir);
50: 
51: 		const event1: RelayMessage = { type: "delta", text: "hello " };
52: 		const event2: RelayMessage = { type: "delta", text: "world" };
53: 		const event3: RelayMessage = { type: "done", code: 0 };
54: 
55: 		cache.recordEvent("session-1", event1);
56: 		cache.recordEvent("session-1", event2);
57: 		cache.recordEvent("session-1", event3);
58: 
59: 		const events = cache.getEvents("session-1");
60: 		expect(events).toHaveLength(3);
61: 		expect(events![0]).toEqual(event1);
62: 		expect(events![1]).toEqual(event2);
63: 		expect(events![2]).toEqual(event3);
64: 	});
65: 
66: 	it("returns null for unknown sessions", () => {
67: 		const cache = new MessageCache(testDir);
68: 		expect(cache.getEvents("nonexistent")).toBeNull();
69: 	});
70: 
71: 	it("records different event types correctly", () => {
72: 		const cache = new MessageCache(testDir);
73: 
74: 		cache.recordEvent("s1", { type: "user_message", text: "hi" });
75: 		cache.recordEvent("s1", { type: "delta", text: "response" });
76: 		cache.recordEvent("s1", { type: "tool_start", id: "t1", name: "Read" });
77: 		cache.recordEvent("s1", {
78: 			type: "tool_executing",
79: 			id: "t1",
80: 			name: "Read",
81: 			input: { path: "foo.ts" },
82: 		});
83: 		cache.recordEvent("s1", {
84: 			type: "tool_result",
85: 			id: "t1",
86: 			content: "file contents",
87: 			is_error: false,
88: 		});
89: 		cache.recordEvent("s1", { type: "thinking_start" });
90: 		cache.recordEvent("s1", { type: "thinking_delta", text: "let me think" });
91: 		cache.recordEvent("s1", { type: "thinking_stop" });
92: 		cache.recordEvent("s1", {
93: 			type: "result",
94: 			usage: { input: 100, output: 50, cache_read: 0, cache_creation: 0 },
95: 			cost: 0.01,
96: 			duration: 1000,
97: 			sessionId: "s1",
98: 		});
99: 		cache.recordEvent("s1", { type: "done", code: 0 });
100: 
101: 		const events = cache.getEvents("s1");
102: 		expect(events).toHaveLength(10);
103: 		expect(events!.map((e) => e.type)).toEqual([
104: 			"user_message",
105: 			"delta",
106: 			"tool_start",
107: 			"tool_executing",
108: 			"tool_result",
109: 			"thinking_start",
110: 			"thinking_delta",
111: 			"thinking_stop",
112: 			"result",
113: 			"done",
114: 		]);
115: 	});
116: });
117: 
118: // ─── JSONL file persistence ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
119: 
120: describe("JSONL file persistence", () => {
121: 	it("writes events to a .jsonl file on disk", () => {
122: 		const cache = new MessageCache(testDir);
123: 
124: 		cache.recordEvent("session-a", { type: "delta", text: "hello" });
125: 		cache.recordEvent("session-a", { type: "done", code: 0 });
126: 
127: 		const filePath = join(testDir, "session-a.jsonl");
128: 		expect(existsSync(filePath)).toBe(true);
129: 
130: 		const content = readFileSync(filePath, "utf8");
131: 		const lines = content.trim().split("\n");
132: 		expect(lines).toHaveLength(2);
133: 		expect(JSON.parse(lines[0]!)).toEqual({ type: "delta", text: "hello" });
134: 		expect(JSON.parse(lines[1]!)).toEqual({ type: "done", code: 0 });
135: 	});
136: 
137: 	it("appends each event as a new line (not rewriting)", () => {
138: 		const cache = new MessageCache(testDir);
139: 
140: 		cache.recordEvent("s1", { type: "delta", text: "a" });
141: 		const contentAfterFirst = readFileSync(join(testDir, "s1.jsonl"), "utf8");
142: 		expect(contentAfterFirst.trim().split("\n")).toHaveLength(1);
143: 
144: 		cache.recordEvent("s1", { type: "delta", text: "b" });
145: 		const contentAfterSecond = readFileSync(join(testDir, "s1.jsonl"), "utf8");
146: 		expect(contentAfterSecond.trim().split("\n")).toHaveLength(2);
147: 	});
148: });
149: 
150: // ─── loadFromDisk ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
151: 
152: describe("loadFromDisk", () => {
153: 	it("recovers events from JSONL files", () => {
154: 		// Manually write a JSONL file
155: 		const filePath = join(testDir, "recovered-session.jsonl");
156: 		const events = [
157: 			{ type: "user_message", text: "hello" },
158: 			{ type: "delta", text: "hi there" },
159: 			{ type: "done", code: 0 },
160: 		];
161: 		writeFileSync(
162: 			filePath,
163: 			`${events.map((e) => JSON.stringify(e)).join("\n")}\n`,
164: 		);
165: 
166: 		// Create a NEW cache instance and load from disk
167: 		const cache = new MessageCache(testDir);
168: 		cache.loadFromDisk();
169:

<content>1: // ─── Per-Session Message Cache ───────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Records every translated event per session in real-time (memory + JSONL file),
3: // replays on session switch through existing client handlers.
4: //
5: // Modeled after claude-relay's doSendAndRecord + appendToSessionFile pattern:
6: //   - Record synchronously before broadcast (identical to claude-relay)
7: //   - Append-only JSONL files (crash-safe, O(1) per event)
8: //   - Fallback chain: memory → file → null (caller uses REST API)
9: 
10: import {
11: 	appendFileSync,
12: 	mkdirSync,
13: 	readdirSync,
14: 	readFileSync,
15: 	unlinkSync,
16: 	writeFileSync,
17: } from "node:fs";
18: import { join } from "node:path";
19: import type { RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
20: 
21: /** Maximum events per session before eviction. */
22: const MAX_EVENTS = 5000;
23: 
24: /** After eviction, keep this fraction of events (newest). */
25: const KEEP_RATIO = 0.8;
26: 
27: // ─── Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28: 
29: export interface FileOpResult {
30: 	ok: boolean;
31: 	error?: string;
32: }
33: 
34: interface SessionCache {
35: 	events: RelayMessage[];
36: 	approxBytes: number;
37: 	lastAccessedAt: number;
38: }
39: 
40: // ─── MessageCache ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
41: 
42: export class MessageCache {
43: 	private readonly sessions = new Map<string, SessionCache>();
44: 	private readonly cacheDir: string;
45: 
46: 	constructor(cacheDir: string) {
47: 		this.cacheDir = cacheDir;
48: 		mkdirSync(cacheDir, { recursive: true });
49: 	}
50: 
51: 	// ── Recording (real-time, identical to doSendAndRecord) ────────────
52: 
53: 	/**
54: 	 * Append event to memory + file. Called synchronously before broadcast.
55: 	 * Identical to claude-relay's doSendAndRecord pattern.
56: 	 * Evicts oldest events when MAX_EVENTS is exceeded.
57: 	 */
58: 	recordEvent(sessionId: string, event: RelayMessage): void {
59: 		const session = this.ensureSession(sessionId);
60: 		session.events.push(event);
61: 
62: 		// Track approximate byte size (UTF-16 estimate)
63: 		const eventBytes = JSON.stringify(event).length * 2;
64: 		session.approxBytes += eventBytes;
65: 		session.lastAccessedAt = Date.now();
66: 
67: 		this.appendToFile(sessionId, event);
68: 
69: 		// Evict oldest events when over limit
70: 		if (session.events.length > MAX_EVENTS) {
71: 			const keepCount = Math.floor(MAX_EVENTS * KEEP_RATIO);
72: 			session.events = session.events.slice(-keepCount);
73: 			// Recompute byte estimate after eviction
74: 			session.approxBytes = JSON.stringify(session.events).length * 2;
75: 			this.rewriteFile(sessionId, session.events);
76: 		}
77: 	}
78: 
79: 	// ── Serving (fallback chain) ───────────────────────────────────────
80: 
81: 	/**
82: 	 * Get raw events for a session. Fallback chain:
83: 	 *   1. In-memory events → return if found
84: 	 *   2. Load from JSONL file on disk → return if found
85: 	 *   3. Return null → caller fetches from OpenCode REST API
86: 	 */
87: 	getEvents(sessionId: string): RelayMessage[] | null {
88: 		// 1. In-memory
89: 		const session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
90: 		if (session && session.events.length > 0) {
91: 			session.lastAccessedAt = Date.now();
92: 			return session.events;
93: 		}
94: 
95: 		// 2. File on disk
96: 		const fromFile = this.loadFromFile(sessionId);
97: 		if (fromFile && fromFile.length > 0) {
98: 			const approxBytes = JSON.stringify(fromFile).length * 2;
99: 			this.sessions.set(sessionId, {
100: 				events: fromFile,
101: 				approxBytes,
102: 				lastAccessedAt: Date.now(),
103: 			});
104: 			return fromFile;
105: 		}
106: 
107: 		// 3. Return null → caller fetches from REST API
108: 		return null;
109: 	}
110: 
111: 	// ── Lifecycle ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
112: 
113: 	/** Remove all data (memory + file). Called on session delete. */
114: 	remove(sessionId: string): void {
115: 		this.sessions.delete(sessionId);
116: 		try {
117: 			unlinkSync(this.filePath(sessionId));
118: 		} catch {
119: 			// File may not exist — that's fine
120: 		}
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	/** Load all .jsonl files into memory. Called once on startup. */
124: 	loadFromDisk(): void {
125: 		let files: string[];
126: 		try {
127: 			files = readdirSync(this.cacheDir);
128: 		} catch {
129: 			return;
130: 		}
131: 
132: 		for (const file of files) {
133: 			if (!file.endsWith(".jsonl")) continue;
134: 			const sessionId = file.slice(0, -6); // Remove ".jsonl"
135: 			const events = this.loadFromFile(sessionId);
136: 			if (events && events.length > 0) {
137: 				const approxBytes = JSON.stringify(events).length * 2;
138: 				this.sessions.set(sessionId, {
139: 					events,
140: 					approxBytes,
141: 					lastAccessedAt: Date.now(),
142: 				});
143: 			}
144: 		}
145: 	}
146: 
147: 	/** Check if a session has cached events in memory. */
148: 	has(sessionId: string): boolean {
149: 		const session = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
150: 		return session !== undefined && session.events.length > 0;
151: 	}
152: 
153: 	/** Number of sessions currently cached. */
154: 	sessionCount(): number {
155: 		return this.sessions.size;
156: 	}
157: 
158: 	/** Total approximate bytes across all cached sessions. */
159: 	approximateBytes(): number {
160: 		let total = 0;
161: 		for (const session of this.sessions.values()) {
162: 			total += session.approxBytes;
163: 		}
164: 		return total;
165: 	}
166: 
167: 	/**
168: 	 * Evict the session with the oldest `lastAccessedAt`.
169: 	 * Removes both memory and disk data.
170: 	 * Returns the evicted session ID, or `null` if no sessions exist.
171: 	 */
172: 	evictOldestSession(): string | null {
173: 		if (this.sessions.size === 0) return null;
174: 
175: 		let oldestId: string | null = null;
176: 		let oldestTime = Infinity;
177: 
178: 		for (const [id, session] of this.sessions) {
179: 			if (session.lastAccessedAt < oldestTime) {
180: 				oldestTime = session.lastAccessedAt;
181: 				oldestId = id;
182: 			}
183: 		}
184: 
185: 		if (oldestId !== null) {
186: 			this.remove(oldestId);
187: 		}
188: 
189: 		return oldestId;
190: 	}
191: 
192: 	// ── Internal ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
193: 
194: 	private ensureSession(sessionId: string): SessionCache {
195: 		const existing = this.sessions.get(sessionId);
196: 		if (existing) return existing;
197: 
198: 		const session: SessionCache = {
199: 			events: [],
200: 			approxBytes: 0,
201: 			lastAccessedAt: Date.now(),
202: 		};
203: 		this.sessions.set(sessionId, session);
204: 		return session;
205: 	}
206: 
207: 	private filePath(sessionId: string): string {
208: 		return join(this.cacheDir, `${sessionId}.jsonl`);
209: 	}
210: 
211: 	private loadFromFile(sessionId: string): RelayMessage[] | null {
212: 		let content: string;
213: 		try {
214: 			content = readFileSync(this.filePath(sessionId), "utf8");
215: 		} catch {
216: 			return null;
217: 		}
218: 
219: 		const lines = content.trim().split("\n");
220: 		if (lines.length === 0 || (lines.length === 1 && lines[0] === "")) {
221: 			return null;
222: 		}
223: 
224: 		const events: RelayMessage[] = [];
225: 		for (const line of lines) {
226: 			if (!line) continue;
227: 			try {
228: 				events.push(JSON.parse(line) as RelayMessage);
229: 			} catch {
230: 				// Skip malformed lines (crash-safe: partial last line is OK)
231: 			}
232: 		}
233: 		return events.length > 0 ? events : null;
234: 	}
235: 
236: 	private appendToFile(sessionId: string, event: RelayMessage): FileOpResult {
237: 		try {
238: 			appendFileSync(this.filePath(sessionId), `${JSON.stringify(event)}\n`);
239: 			return { ok: true };
240: 		} catch (err) {
241: 			return { ok: false, error: (err as Error).message };
242: 		}
243: 	}
244: 
245: 	/** Rewrite the JSONL file with only the given events (after eviction). */
246: 	private rewriteFile(sessionId: string, events: RelayMessage[]): FileOpResult {
247: 		try {
248: 			const content = `${events.map((e) => JSON.stringify(e)).join("\n")}\n`;
249: 			writeFileSync(this.filePath(sessionId), content);
250: 			return { ok: true };
251: 		} catch (err) {
252: 			return { ok: false, error: (err as Error).message };
253: 		}
254: 	}
255: }
